title: Leptogastrodia peichatieniana
intro: Leptogastrodia peichatieniana, also known as Pei Chai, is a rare and endangered species of orchid native to Taiwan. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including hypertension, diabetes, and inflammation, due to its potential to lower blood pressure, improve insulin sensitivity, and exhibit anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Leptogastrodia peichatieniana include iridoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional Chinese medicine, Leptogastrodia peichatieniana is commonly prepared into decoctions, extracts, or tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health issues, including cardiovascular and metabolic disorders.
